d13e7c01-dbf0-484d-9d4b-f9fe32448634.author: 79Ig
d13e7c01-dbf0-484d-9d4b-f9fe32448634.content: Discovered while working #76. mdatron verify uses VerifyConfig::new() which hardcodes file_globs=['**/*.md']; the CLI never reads the committed .mdatron/config.yaml. So 'mdatron verify --project-root .' walks ALL 54 .claude/*.md + .crosslink/*.md tooling files plus review-log/, not just mdatron's own typed-markdown corpus. It stays clean ONLY because those files currently have valid (or absent) YAML frontmatter — proven fragile: a local .claude/commands/design.md (Claude Code skill file, argument-hint line is not strict YAML) makes verify emit E0001. Impact: #46's self-validate CI gate would break the moment any committed non-corpus .md carries non-YAML frontmatter — and 'verify via its own .mdatron/ config' (#46 acceptance) is not actually honored since config.yaml is inert. FIX: (1) cmd_verify reads .mdatron/config.yaml file_globs (fall back to default when absent); (2) set the committed config.yaml globs to scope the corpus (review-log/**/*.md) so verify validates only mdatron-owned typed-markdown. NOTE: config.yaml is a managed/drift-guarded file (init manifest) but file_globs are inherently project-specific — this resurfaces the config-governance question (adopter-customizable config vs engine-managed). Links #46/#44. Correcting my earlier #46 assessment: I concluded 'default globs scope correctly (glob skips dotdirs)' — that was WRONG; the glob DOES walk dotdirs, it just happened to find valid frontmatter.

e085cc76-85b1-41b6-b405-1151fea8f69c.author: 79Ig
e085cc76-85b1-41b6-b405-1151fea8f69c.content: CONSUMER RAISE (vsdd-cli relay 2026-07-22, Raise 3) converges on this issue from the consumer side — same design.md incident, seen as a BLOCKED-COMMITS event on their estate: crosslink 0.9's migration deployed a command file with non-strict-YAML frontmatter; mdatron's walk refused it (correctly by its own rules) and blocked the consuming estate's commits over a document mdatron has no mandate to govern. Their asks, in their preference order: (a) ROUTE-TABLE ALLOWLIST — mdatron validates only what a consumer explicitly routes, everything else untouched (they cite Thermite's spec-routes.toml as our own closed-world precedent); (b) ignore list as lighter interim; (c) a stated default — route-only being the closed-world-correct choice, retiring this incident's whole class (an unrouted third-party command file isn't mdatron's to refuse). ASSESSMENT: (a) and my original fix here are the same mechanism — honoring .mdatron/config.yaml file_globs IS the consumer-authored allowlist; the residual decision is the DEFAULT when config is absent (current walk-all vs route-only/empty). Route-only-by-default is a behavior change wanting an SO scope ruling + DESIGN language; honoring file_globs is a straight defect fix (config is committed, deployed by init, and inert). Priority raised: this blocked a consumer's commits in the wild.

b788e5c7-7900-4c9a-9717-09928a125c2a.content: Pre-existing flake, unrelated to #46 (surfaced on #46's CI where zero Rust changed). confine.rs:781 asserts 'after <= before + 2' where before/after are process-GLOBAL open-FD counts (open_fd_count reads /proc/self/fd or equivalent). cargo runs lib tests multi-threaded, so any concurrent test opening an FD during the 300-op measurement window perturbs the count and trips the +2 tolerance. Passed on first CI run + macOS this run; failed ubuntu once; 5/5 locally. Proposed fix: keep the leak signal meaningful (a real 1-FD/op leak over 300 ops = +300) while tolerating parallel noise -- serialize FD-sensitive tests behind a static Mutex AND widen the tolerance to a value well below the op count but above realistic parallel noise (e.g. +16 or +32). Test-only change; confine is security-labelled so SE+Security review per fix-lane. Low priority (intermittent, does not affect the confinement code itself).

42c59e2e-93f1-4773-a248-dde7c2390bed.author: 79Ig
42c59e2e-93f1-4773-a248-dde7c2390bed.content: From #60 OBS-1 (vsdd-cli field report). When frontmatter schema validation (MDATRON-E0050) fails, the diagnostic reports the frontmatter block's line (line 1) plus a JSON pointer (e.g. /action_vocabulary/20). Correct, but on a 40+ line frontmatter the adopter still maps the pointer to the offending source line by hand. ASK: also resolve the pointer to its source line. SIZE (assessed): non-trivial — schema.rs carries only instance_path (the pointer, from jsonschema); the frontmatter YAML parse would need to retain per-node line/span info and the pointer resolved against it to recover the line. Needs a location-tracking YAML parse (serde_yaml doesn't expose spans cleanly) or a second marked pass. Its own fix-lane. Linked from #60.

4e252f96-e051-46f3-94ba-4380b6ed9618.author: 79Ig
4e252f96-e051-46f3-94ba-4380b6ed9618.content: SCOPED-HYBRID EVALUATION (post-#69 migration to serde_yaml_ng). serde_yaml_ng is a serde_yaml fork, so it carries the SAME position-less Value — #65 is still unsolved by the migration. THE HYBRID: keep serde_yaml_ng for the happy-path typed parse/validation, and add a MARKED parser used ONLY on the error path — when schema validation fails at a JSON pointer (e.g. /action_vocabulary/20), re-parse the frontmatter with the marked parser, walk the pointer path (mapping key -> seq index) to the node, read its Marker{line,col}, and offset by the frontmatter block's start line to get the file line. FEASIBILITY: sound. Use saphyr / saphyr-parser (mature, pure-Rust, MIT/Apache) which exposes MarkedYaml/Marker; the pointer->node walk is ~50-100 lines + tests. Error-path only, so ZERO happy-path cost. COST: (a) a SECOND YAML parser dependency (saphyr) alongside serde_yaml_ng — another SO+PE+Security approval; (b) resolution logic + edge cases (anchors/aliases, multi-line scalars, schema-path vs instance-path pointers). RISK vs ALTERNATIVES: lower-risk than adopting serde-saphyr (0.0.8-alpha) as the sole parser today; and using saphyr-PARSER (not the alpha serde-saphyr layer) is a stepping stone toward a future single-parser solution when serde-saphyr matures. RECOMMENDATION: #65 is a low-priority DX nicety (the diagnostic already carries the precise JSON pointer). DEFER until either source-lines are wanted soon (then do the hybrid with saphyr-parser) OR serde-saphyr leaves alpha (then one clean parser gives typed-parse + spans + budget, potentially replacing serde_yaml_ng too). No action needed now; #65 stays open with this path documented.

59bc0bb7-814f-4c44-af8d-7942eebde8fd.author: 79Ig
59bc0bb7-814f-4c44-af8d-7942eebde8fd.content: PASS RED-TEAM: could not force a wrong line or misbehavior. Adversarial pointers by construction all degrade safely: escaped /a-tilde1-b decodes to key a/b (matches); out-of-range /tags/9 returns None (unit test); a numeric token against a mapping, or a non-string YAML key, does not match and returns None; empty pointer returns root; a lone slash returns None; no frontmatter returns None. Every miss falls back to (1,0); none panic; none yield a confidently-wrong line. The only surprise, value-on-a-different-line-than-its-key (block or nested scalars), resolves to the VALUE line which is semantically correct, and structural errors land at the mapping start (a limitation, not misdirection). Red gate confirmed genuine: the pre-image hardcoded line:1 column:0 (per the diff), and schema_violation_reports_source_line_not_block_start asserts location.line == 6, so against the old code it fails left:1 right:6 (matches the recorded driver-lane values); it passes post-change in my run. Independent gates actuals: cargo test --workspace all green across 7 suites (main 5, cli_integration 22, output_format 10, core lib 188 including the 4 resolver unit tests and the E0050 integration test, phase_1_contracts 12, red_gate_retrofit 13, doc-tests 1; 0 failed); cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -D warnings exit 0 with no warnings; cargo audit exit 0 with no vulnerabilities.

64e19885-ce73-43ad-a4a7-b4afcb216a66.author: 79Ig
64e19885-ce73-43ad-a4a7-b4afcb216a66.content: PASS SECURITY (dependency + parse surface): saphyr 0.0.11 is error-path only. grep confirms saphyr usage is confined to the frontmatter.rs resolver and the sole non-test caller is verify.rs:283 in the schema-violation loop. No unsafe added (none in frontmatter.rs). New transitive deps (saphyr, saphyr-parser, arraydeque, ordered-float, hashlink, encoding_rs, foldhash) scanned by cargo audit: exit 0, no advisories (139 crate deps, 1166 advisories loaded). DoS reasoning holds: saphyr only re-parses a block that serde_yaml_ng ALREADY accepted into a Value (verify_file gates E0050 behind parse() returning Ok), so it is not adversarial-first-contact and is bounded by what the happy-path parser survived. Residual risk: saphyr is pre-1.0 (0.0.x); a panic rather than Err on some edge input would propagate because only Err is caught (load_from_str(...).ok()?). Low and accepted: error-path only, behind the resolver seam, inputs pre-filtered by serde_yaml_ng; matches the recorded SO+PE+Security approval (comment -117).

67f38bce-ef72-4322-a06e-a12beb5c966f.author: 79Ig
67f38bce-ef72-4322-a06e-a12beb5c966f.content: VALUE REASSESSMENT via the AI-Engineer domain + Thermite (operator asked: is saphyr-parser's source-line benefit high-value for the AGENT?). VERDICT: HIGH value — reframed up from the earlier 'low-pri human DX nicety.' GROUNDING: (1) THERMITE precedent — its failed proof obligations pair a / with the concrete diagnostic ('counterexamples, not adjectives', RATIONALE §5.1); its lower errors carry ; the agent edit loop runs off the location ('the hole's span, re-parses, re-checks'). mdatron's marking discipline descends from Thermite (DESIGN § Structural inspiration), so location-carrying diagnostics ARE the reference pattern. (2) AI-ENGINEER domain (per-task agent cost + bounded scope) — a bare JSON pointer forces the consuming agent into a localize-then-fix loop: re-read file, parse YAML, COUNT to array element 20 to find the line, then edit. That is extra tool calls + tokens AND a classic LLM failure mode (miscounting a deep array index -> edits the WRONG line). A  collapses it to a direct edit. Recurs on EVERY schema violation at hook/pre-commit time where the agent is the fixer; DESIGN § 'Agents are the first consumer' makes the agent the primary consumer. VALUE SCALES with frontmatter depth/arrays (vsdd-cli's live catch was /action_vocabulary/20 in a 40+ line block — the exact high-cost case). NET: saphyr-parser's benefit is genuinely high for agent actionability + cost + error-rate, not cosmetic. RE-PRIORITIZE low -> medium. Remaining gate unchanged: the saphyr-parser dependency approval (SO+PE+Security) — but now with a strong domain-backed value case. Implementation path = the recorded hybrid (serde_yaml_ng typed parse + saphyr-parser marked reparse on the error path).

9816f471-f3bd-4e9c-93e1-1276b1e42700.author: 79Ig
9816f471-f3bd-4e9c-93e1-1276b1e42700.content: PASS AI-ENGINEER (value confirm): delivers the agent-actionability value. verify.rs:282-284 resolves the pointer to (line,column) and the message still carries the pointer (ve.message plus ve.instance_path), so the fixing agent gets file:line for a direct edit AND the pointer for context, instead of counting to array element N by hand. Error-path only: resolve_pointer_location has exactly one non-test caller, verify.rs:283, inside the for ve in schema.validate(...) loop; the happy path frontmatter::parse is untouched, so zero happy-path cost (confirmed by grep). Cost note: it re-parses the block once PER violation (yaml_block plus MarkedYaml::load_from_str each iteration), so N violations in one file cost N marked parses. Acceptable on the error path and bounded by frontmatter size; a straightforward future optimization is to build the marked tree once per file and reuse it across violations. Not blocking.

e474f469-d6dc-4650-bc86-567bbd00d09a.author: 79Ig
e474f469-d6dc-4650-bc86-567bbd00d09a.content: Cold review of #65 (commit ddb178d, E0050 source-line resolution via error-path-only saphyr reparse). 4 lens verdicts recorded, all PASS: SOFTWARE-ENGINEER (walk_pointer all-Option/panic-free, RFC-6901 unescape order correct, offset math guarded and empirically validated), AI-ENGINEER (agent-actionability confirmed, error-path-only zero happy-path cost; minor note: one saphyr parse per violation, cache-per-file is a future optimization), SECURITY (saphyr error-path-only, no unsafe added, cargo audit clean, DoS bounded because serde_yaml_ng gates the reparse), RED-TEAM (no wrong-line or panic reproducible; red gate genuinely fails against old line:1 left:1 right:6). e4b344dd-4e7b-48e5-bb65-f9c02b083673.author: 79Ig
e4b344dd-4e7b-48e5-bb65-f9c02b083673.content: PASS SOFTWARE-ENGINEER (owner): resolve_pointer_location + walk_pointer are correct and panic-free. walk_pointer is all-Option: token.parse::<usize>().ok()?, seq.get(idx)?, map.iter().find_map(...)?, and the fallthrough returns None; no unwrap and no index-panic. Offset math marker.line()+1 is guarded by u32::try_from(...).ok()? and checked_add(1); column uses saturating_add(1). The RFC-6901 unescape runs in the required order (replace tilde1 to slash THEN tilde0 to tilde) so a token like tilde-0-1 decodes to tilde1 rather than slash. Line correctness validated empirically by the tests: /title to 2, /tags/1 to 5, /count to 6, and /phase to 6 in the integration test. Empty pointer returns root (block start); unresolvable and out-of-range return None so the caller falls back to (1,0) at verify.rs:284. A mapping token resolves to the VALUE node, so E0050 lands on the value line, which is correct for wrong-value violations. Caveat (not a defect): for required and additionalProperties the jsonschema instance_path is the parent object, so those land at the mapping start (about line 2) rather than the exact offending line, still strictly better than the old hardcoded 1. Minor latent note: a pointer lacking a leading slash would resolve to root (loop body skipped), but jsonschema always emits RFC-6901 pointers so it is unreachable.

f030433d-b819-4153-b9d2-dfd3ea77a8bc.author: 79Ig
f030433d-b819-4153-b9d2-dfd3ea77a8bc.content: FEASIBILITY (assessed on main). BLOCKED on a dependency-approval decision. The pipeline: frontmatter::parse -> serde_yaml::Value (0.9, position-LESS) -> yaml_to_json (serde interconversion, discards any node->source mapping) -> jsonschema validate -> ValidationError.instance_path (the JSON pointer). There is no path from the pointer back to a source line with the current parser: serde_yaml::Value retains no marks. Mapping the pointer to a line REQUIRES a mark-aware YAML parse (a second pass with saphyr / yaml-rust2, which expose per-node Marker{line,col}), resolving the pointer against the marked tree, then offsetting by the frontmatter block's start line. That is a NEW dependency — and per this project's dependency-approval discipline (the SEC-F5 finding on #57 was exactly an unapproved dep; PE domain dimension 2 / VSDD-E0100), adding it needs SO+PE+Security sign-off with an investigation record BEFORE implementation. So #65 is gated on that decision, not on implementation effort. Recommend: if approved, saphyr (maintained successor to yaml-rust2, MIT/Apache) used ONLY for the diagnostic pointer->line resolution on the error path (happy path stays serde_yaml). Operator decision needed.

31f4d73d-f1e9-4ac1-864d-736d444ade9a.title: process: L2 confinement fix implemented outside phase discipline — deviation record + routing rule for ratification
31f4d73d-f1e9-4ac1-864d-736d444ade9a.description: Deviation record. Issue L2 (path-confinement defect, security-labeled) was fixed direct-to-main at 0b21986 as real engine implementation: a new confine module (unsafe openat walk, OwnedFd handling), the index.rs read-path rewiring, a newly emitted diagnostic code (E0012), a new dependency (libc), and its test suite. VSDD artifacts used: session work set, typed plan/result comments, commit evidence. VSDD artifacts NOT used: phase-2a red gate (tests were authored with the implementation, never demonstrated failing against the defect first), phase-2b/2c primers, software-engineer domain review, security domain review (despite the security label), rust supplemental skill, and backlog linkage (the work was picked up and implemented without first being tied to its owning layer issue and milestone). The fix was defensible as current-code maintenance — the defect predates the ratified design — but it also front-ran phase-2 decomposition of the ratified snapshot/confinement contracts (validated no-follow handles, component-wise resolution), i.e. increment implementation without increment gates. Why it matters: review config is 'thorough — the engine gates other projects' work; its defects propagate', and an unreviewed hotfix lane is the process form of silent degradation. Proposed routing rule (operator ratifies, then it becomes a standing ruling recorded per the document-lifecycle discipline): any code-touching work in this repo, defect fixes included, requires before implementation (1) a tracker issue linked to its owning layer issue and milestone; (2) phase-2a red gate — the failing test demonstrated against the defect before the fix lands; (3) the domain reviews the labels mandate (software-engineer minimum; security label adds the security domain); (4) the language supplemental skill for the implementation language. No emergency bypass lane: pre-v1, nothing is urgent enough to skip the gates; post-v1, a bypass must be its own loud, annotated ruling. Child issue carries the retroactive verification debt for 0b21986.

49197eb8-d2b6-4462-9185-1e9065d2d847.title: Path-confinement helper: fallback and starts_with flaws; traversal test passes only via macOS symlink
49197eb8-d2b6-4462-9185-1e9065d2d847.description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

Found by the security cold review of the conformance-engine spec (issue #1, round 1). Evidence: index.rs:159-168 — confine_under_root canonicalizes both sides but on a non-existent target falls back to the un-normalized textual path (unwrap_or_else(|_| path.to_path_buf())), and PathBuf::starts_with is component-wise, so root/../.. can pass. The passing test path_traversal_via_parent_dots_is_rejected (index.rs:532-541) succeeds only because macOS temp_dir is a symlink (/var -> /private/var), making canonical root and textual path diverge; on a platform without that symlink the .. case would not be rejected. Fix: normalize without requiring existence (component-wise resolution), reject on any remaining parent-dir component, and add non-symlink-dependent traversal tests. Current-code defect, independent of the spec draft.

125c7752-515e-4789-80e5-900f487478a6.author: 79Ig
125c7752-515e-4789-80e5-900f487478a6.content: Fixed at commit 0b21986 (plus 5e00793, a mechanical rustfmt sweep of pre-existing drift kept separate from the fix diff). confine_under_root is gone; confinement is now two-layered in the new mdatron-core confine module: confine_lexically() rejects absolute paths and parent segments on the path text component-wise before any filesystem access — so the non-existent-target fallback hole and the starts_with component comparison are both structurally eliminated, and glob patterns are confined before expansion — and open_confined() walks each component via openat with O_NOFOLLOW from the parent directory's handle (macOS reports symlink-to-dir under O_DIRECTORY as ENOTDIR; disambiguated with handle-relative fstatat AT_SYMLINK_NOFOLLOW), so a symlinked intermediate component is refused like a symlinked final one and index parsing reads from the very handle that passed confinement (check-then-read gap closed for key() sources). Codes: E0011 stays parent-traversal, E0010 (absolute) and E0012 (symlink-refused, new in-range allocation) now emitted; explain pages for all three added per the one-entry-per-emitted-code rule — flagging for phase-1b code-range ratification. Traversal tests no longer depend on macOS /var symlink divergence: non-existent escape target, existent escape target (sibling file really present), interior non-escaping parent segment, absolute spellings (existent + non-existent), and glob-with-parent-segment are all rejected lexically; unix symlink tests cover leaf (target inside AND outside root), intermediate directory, and glob-matched symlinks; confined-but-missing source asserted as Io, not traversal. Evidence: cargo test --workspace 217 passed / 0 failed; fmt --check clean; clippy zero warnings; explain MDATRON-E0012 and explain --json MDATRON-E0010 exercised via the binary.

70fcbc23-8927-4169-a3e9-0b81b49014b5.author: 79Ig
70fcbc23-8927-4169-a3e9-0b81b49014b5.content: Fix design, per DESIGN.md § Five check families (path confinement) and § Verification is fast where it is invoked (snapshot reads): (1) new mdatron-core confine module — confine_lexically() decides confinement textually (absolute-path and parent-segment rejection over components, CurDir skipped), so non-existent targets are judged on the same basis as existing ones and the starts_with/fallback hole closes; (2) open_confined() opens each component relative to its parent directory handle with O_NOFOLLOW (openat loop on unix, libc), so a symlinked intermediate component is refused exactly like a symlinked final one and the returned handle is the handle the parser reads — confinement decided on the handle, closing the check-then-read gap for key() sources; (3) index.rs rewired: lexical check before glob expansion (glob patterns may not contain parent segments, per absorbed BOUNDARY-PREAMBLE § 7), reads go through the confined handle, IndexError grows AbsoluteSource (E0010) and SymlinkRefused (E0012, new in-range allocation, catalog entry added) beside PathTraversal (E0011); (4) explain pages for E0010/E0011/E0012 land per the one-entry-per-emitted-code rule; (5) non-symlink-dependent traversal tests: parent-dot with non-existent target, interior parent segments, absolute source (existent + non-existent), glob-with-parent-segment; plus unix symlink tests for leaf and intermediate components. libc added as unix-only dep with stated reason (no std openat surface).

700bbf38-a0db-46e2-90a8-36df989ecb06.title: confine: open_confined silently sanitizes non-Normal components instead of rejecting them

26dfc82b-65df-412a-a788-07cb6196fa76.author: i8fm
26dfc82b-65df-412a-a788-07cb6196fa76.content:
RED-TEAM review of f13fb61: PASS — could not defeat the fix.

Attempts and why each fails:
1. Construct a ConfinedPath carrying ../root: confine_lexically (confine.rs:79-84) returns early Err on the first Prefix/RootDir/ParentDir; no arm pushes a non-Normal. Impossible.
2. Bypass the constructor: the field is private (confine.rs:37); external code cannot write ConfinedPath(..) nor read .0.
3. as_path() round-trip: yields &Path only; rebuilding "../x" as a PathBuf cannot be wrapped back except via confine_lexically, which rejects it.
4. Glob smuggling: pattern base is pre-confined; wildcards cannot expand to ..; and each match is strip_prefix'd + re-confined (index.rs:197-205), so any residual .. / root / outside-root match becomes PathTraversal, never an open. Symlinked matches die at open_confined's no-follow walk.
5. Empty / "."-only path: confine_lexically drops CurDir => empty PathBuf => open_confined returns Io (empty source, confine.rs:124-128); no file opened, no traversal.
6. Symlink TOCTOU: open_confined_impl (unchanged by this commit) opens each component with openat + O_NOFOLLOW; the lexical layer touches no filesystem, so there is no check-then-read path gap to swap.
7. Trigger unreachable!() (confine.rs:119) for a wrong open: it panics (fail-closed), never opens a wrong file — strictly safer than the pre-fix silent sanitize.

compile_fail DOCTEST IS GENUINE, NOT A FALSE GREEN. I compiled the exact doctest body out-of-tree against mdatron-core and it fails with EXACTLY the stated reason:
  error[E0308]: mismatched types
    | let _ = open_confined(Path::new("/tmp"), Path::new("../secret.yaml"));
    |         arguments to this function are incorrect / expected &ConfinedPath, found &Path
The import `use mdatron_core::confine::open_confined;` RESOLVES (confine is a pub mod, open_confined is pub), so the failure is the type mismatch itself, not an unrelated unresolved-import error. Runtime seam also holds: red_gate_parent_traversal_cannot_reach_open confirms confine_lexically rejects "../secret.yaml" as ParentSegment while "secret.yaml" still opens.

GATES verified independently on a working toolchain (this host has cc/ld/cargo, unlike the container lane): cargo test -p mdatron-core = 195 pass (incl. the compile_fail doctest); cargo clippy -- -D warnings = clean. The only fmt drift is tests/red_gate_retrofit.rs:56/191/210 — a file f13fb61 does not touch (git show --name-only lists only confine.rs + dsl/index.rs); inherited from main, routed to #57, not this commit's.

40443d53-127a-451d-8963-3fa28e3f5f90.author: i8fm
40443d53-127a-451d-8963-3fa28e3f5f90.content: Driver-lane verification (host-side; container agent Qx3p-YMIR authored the fix but could not verify in-container — no linker, dangling gitdir, #59). d3c4f730-d8e8-4e17-8ce9-d3eea0a08c1f.author: i8fm
d3c4f730-d8e8-4e17-8ce9-d3eea0a08c1f.content:
SOFTWARE ENGINEER review of f13fb61: PASS — the newtype closes SE-F1 at the type level.

SE-F1 was: open_confined filter_mapped RootDir/Prefix/ParentDir away, so a caller skipping confine_lexically got silent wrong-file access. The fix changes the signature to open_confined(root: &Path, rel: &ConfinedPath) (confine.rs:110); a raw &Path no longer type-checks — verified out-of-tree, E0308 "expected &ConfinedPath, found &Path" (evidence in the red-team comment).

INVARIANT ESTABLISHED AT CONSTRUCTION. confine_lexically (confine.rs:76-87) is exhaustive over Component: Prefix/RootDir => return Err(Absolute), ParentDir => return Err(ParentSegment), CurDir => dropped, Normal => pushed. It returns early on the first violation and only ever pushes Normal components, so every component of the wrapped PathBuf is Normal. Guarded by leading_parent_segment_rejected / interior_parent_segment_rejected / absolute_path_rejected / deep_traversal_rejected.

INVARIANT IS IMMUTABLE. The field ConfinedPath(PathBuf) (confine.rs:37) is private to the module; the sole accessor as_path(&self) -> &Path (confine.rs:42) is a shared borrow. There is no as_mut / into_inner / DerefMut / Default / From; the derived Clone preserves the invariant. No post-construction mutation path exists.

SOLE CONSTRUCTOR. confine_lexically is the only code that builds ConfinedPath(..); the private field blocks direct construction (and any read of .0) from outside the confine module. Nothing else can mint one.

unreachable!() (confine.rs:119-121) IS GENUINELY UNREACHABLE. rel.0 was assembled solely from Normal pushes, and re-parsing it via .components() reclassifies nothing on the supported unix surface: '.' / '..' can never appear as a Normal (they parse as CurDir/ParentDir before ever being pushed), and ':' etc. are ordinary filename bytes with no Prefix concept on unix. The empty-path case yields an empty component vec, caught by the is_empty() guard (confine.rs:124) before the map is consumed. Even in a hypothetical re-parse divergence the arm panics (fail-closed) rather than opening a wrong file — strictly safer than the pre-fix silent drop. I found no way to obtain a ConfinedPath carrying .. or a root component.

Gate (independently, working toolchain): cargo test -p mdatron-core green — 169 lib + 12 + 13 + 1 doctest = 195 pass.

d806b72a-8aba-41d2-af37-05593dd04de6.author: i8fm
d806b72a-8aba-41d2-af37-05593dd04de6.content:
SOLUTION ARCHITECT review of f13fb61 (API seam): PASS.

RIGHT SEAM / MINIMAL SURFACE. ConfinedPath (confine.rs:37) is an opaque newtype — private PathBuf field, single read-only accessor as_path(&self) -> &Path (confine.rs:42). The public surface added is exactly one type plus one borrow. No into_inner / as_mut_path / DerefMut, so a caller cannot mutate the inner path and cannot reconstruct an unconfined instance: as_path() yields a &Path you may copy into an arbitrary PathBuf, but the only route back to ConfinedPath is confine_lexically, which re-validates. The raw PathBuf never leaks in a way that defeats the invariant.

BOTH CALL SITES IN dsl/index.rs PORTED CORRECTLY.
- resolve_source returns Vec<confine::ConfinedPath> (index.rs:164-167); literal branch pushes the confine_lexically result unchanged (index.rs:209).
- build_index (index.rs:139-141) iterates ConfinedPath, uses rel.as_path() for the display/error string only, and passes &rel to open_confined. Correct.

GLOB-BRANCH RE-CONFINEMENT IS SOUND (index.rs:192-206). The pattern base is project_root.join(rel.as_path()) where rel already passed confine_lexically, so it carries no .. or root. Each match is strip_prefix(project_root)'d (index.rs:197-201 — a match outside the root, e.g. the F2/#54 sibling-tree pathology, fails here and surfaces as PathTraversal, never a read) and then re-confined via confine_lexically(stripped) (index.rs:202-205), which rejects any residual .. or root before a ConfinedPath is minted. Glob wildcards (* ? [) cannot expand to .. and the crate does not match . / .. entries, so the matched-leaf set is unchanged; symlinked matches remain caught downstream at open_confined's no-follow walk (glob_matched_symlink_refused stays green). No traversal can be smuggled past strip_prefix + re-confine.

BEHAVIORAL PARITY. The pushed path is the same root-relative path as before, now wrapped and re-validated; CurDir components are dropped and there are none in normal matches, so the enumerated set does not change. clippy -D warnings is clean (verified here); the orphaned `use std::path::PathBuf` the container agent left behind was correctly narrowed to Path at the top of the module (index.rs:31) and moved into the test module that still needs it (index.rs:414).

7b3cace9-64b2-4af6-95b2-7f27e9a10087.author: 79Ig
7b3cace9-64b2-4af6-95b2-7f27e9a10087.content:
Four pending operator/SO decisions, batched for one ratification pass. Each has full context on its source issue; this issue is the single home now that #77/#78 are closed with their code shipped.

D1 — VERIFY DEFAULT when config.yaml is absent (#77 residual, consumer preference: route-only). Current: walk-all (**/*.md). Route-only is closed-world-correct (Thermite spec-routes.toml precedent) and retires the unrouted-third-party-file class entirely, but is a behavior change for config-less trees (verify would validate nothing until globs are declared — arguably correct: no jurisdiction declared, nothing governed). NOTE: init seeds config.yaml with **/*.md, so any init'd tree has explicit globs; the default only bites non-init'd trees.

D2 — LOUDNESS for routed-but-frontmatterless files (#78 ask b). A file matching a governed glob (or a configured must-have-frontmatter glob) that parses to Ok(None) currently passes silently — indistinguishable from validated-clean. Proposal: a W-code in the config/governance range (W0040-99) e.g. MDATRON-W0040 'governed-file-has-no-frontmatter', opt-in via config to avoid blanket noise on prose-only md. Needs DESIGN language.

D3 — NUMERIC-CLAIMS family scope (#79). First body-prose primitive vs the deliberately-narrowed DSL; DESIGN gates body-content extraction behind a falsifiability run at the 80% bar (DESIGN L85), so ratifying scope likely also schedules a falsifiability increment (#47 synergy). Conservative first cut: configured field references only; vsdd contributes 3 historical drift fixtures.

D4 — COMPACT per-finding SIZE-LIMIT number (#44, deferred from phase 1b alongside the mutation floor). A contract limit, not a band from actuals (DESIGN L29). Strawman for ratification: 512 bytes per finding in compact form (fits code+summary+location+one marked quoted line for typical findings; ~an order of magnitude under typical hook context budgets); truncation at line boundary + elision marker per DESIGN L30. The mutation-testing kill-ratio floor (also phase-1b-deferred) could ride the same ruling if desired.

Rulings can be dropped as comments here; each then routes to its build lane (D1/D2 small; D3 = #45 design pass; D4 unblocks #44 compact-verify).

Passed their ratified scope test (document-anchored, generic), failed their support test, raised here. DEFECT: frontmatter.rs parse() requires content to start exactly '---\n' (~line 27). A UTF-8 BOM (U+FEFF) prefix or CRLF fence ('---\r\n') fails the check -> Ok(None) -> verify's dispatch finds no schema_class (verify.rs ~269-277), no schema and no pattern applies -> the file PASSES THE WALK SILENTLY. Meanwhile vsdd's lenient consumer-side loader (tolerates BOM+CRLF after a review finding) validates the same file — a governed artifact validated by one tool, invisible to the other. The hole sits precisely on Windows-editor provenance (and we ship a windows CI leg — CRLF is in scope). KEY ASYMMETRY: parse FAILURE is loud (E0001, proven live this week); parse ABSENCE is indistinguishable from not-governed. THREE SEPARABLE ASKS: (a) tolerate leading BOM + CRLF fences in parse() — straight fix, this issue's core; (b) loudness option: a file matching a governed glob (or configured must-have-frontmatter glob) that parses Ok(None) reports instead of silently passing — interacts with #77's routing (a route table makes 'routed but frontmatterless' well-defined); needs a new W-code + design language, likely SO-touching; (c) shared fence-edge conformance corpus both projects test against — vsdd contributes: BOM opening, CRLF fences, bare '---' inside a block scalar (BOTH parsers truncate at first bare fence — pin as shared deliberate semantics), empty block, unterminated fence. PLAN: fix (a) with red gates from the (c) corpus cases in one lane; route (b) to a design decision alongside #77's default question.

3495984e-1e52-442e-8494-e6b5d49e72ff.author: 79Ig
3495984e-1e52-442e-8494-e6b5d49e72ff.content:
RESOLVED (assessed; findings dispatched). Complete disposition of vsdd-cli's declared cross-field constraints against mdatron's CURRENT capability — the outcome is that nearly all are expressible TODAY, no immediate mdatron build required:

- REFERENTIAL INTEGRITY (pairs_with/referenced_by/recovery_action/relevant-domain resolution): Layer 2 DSL TODAY via every(x in ..., defined(key(index, x))) — the shape vsdd-cli already runs in cross-references.yaml. RELAY: wire these up now; they never awaited mdatron.
- CONDITIONAL PREFIX ('phase-kind member id leads with the stage designator, phase-1a -> 1a-'): Layer 1 JSON Schema if/then + pattern (draft 2020-12, which mdatron compiles) — TODAY, no mdatron change; it belongs in Layer 1, not the narrowed DSL.
- ARRAY UNIQUENESS ('ids unique within each list'): Layer 1 uniqueItems (supported) — TODAY. Caveat: uniqueItems is whole-item uniqueness; for id-field-only uniqueness within objects it holds when objects differ by id (the normal case).
- EXACT-COVERAGE ('covers exactly {absent, malformed, permission-or-io}', 'exactly one carries lane'): Layer 2 DSL via difference()+count() and count-of-filtered — TODAY, if a touch verbose.
- REGEX-VALIDITY ('branch patterns valid regexes'): the ONE genuine gap. mdatron's schema.rs compiles with Draft202012 but does NOT enable format assertion, so format:regex is annotation-only (not enforced). Options, both DEFERRED to an operator/SO decision (neither is a slam-dunk): (a) enable jsonschema should_validate_formats — a BEHAVIOR CHANGE (schemas using format keywords would begin asserting; compat risk for existing adopters); (b) a DSL regex-valid function — a DSL expansion against the deliberately-narrowed scope. Recommend leaving both deferred; regex-validity is a niche constraint and vsdd-cli can validate it consumer-side meanwhile.

NET: mdatron needs no code change to serve vsdd-cli's first-consumer constraints except the deferred regex-validity decision. Requirement captured for the cross-file family roadmap (#45). Closing.

888ca36a-a976-4a2d-b235-395e1e06af5c.author: 79Ig
888ca36a-a976-4a2d-b235-395e1e06af5c.content:
Actioning #60's cross-repo radar item (vsdd-cli field report): the declared-but-not-yet-executed cross-field constraints in vsdd-cli's schema descriptions, assessed against mdatron's CURRENT Layer 2 DSL (functions: count/len/defined/union/intersect/difference/concat/join/key/every/some; NO string-prefix, regex, or distinct). vsdd-cli's constraints (from .mdatron/schemas/*.json descriptions + the existing .mdatron/patterns/cross-references.yaml) split three ways:

EXECUTABLE TODAY (referential integrity) — the majority. 'every id in pairs_with resolves to an entry id', 'every referenced_by resolves to a reference_surfaces id', 'each recovery_action resolves to a recovery-family member', 'action_vocabulary phase is empty or a scope_members id'. All are the every(x in .field, defined(key(index, x))) shape vsdd-cli ALREADY runs (cross-references.yaml VSDD-E0202/0203/0204). RELAY: vsdd-cli can wire these up now; they do not await new mdatron capability. Only nuance: within-file self-reference (pairs_with -> an entry id in THIS manifest) needs the index to include the file's own entries.

PARTIALLY EXPRESSIBLE / AWKWARD — uniqueness ('ids unique within each list', 'field names unique') and exact-coverage ('covers exactly {absent, malformed, permission-or-io}', 'exactly one carries kind lane', 'exactly the contract's five/six'). Set-equality via difference()+count() covers 'exactly {set}'; 'exactly one' via count of a filtered set. Uniqueness has no clean primitive (no distinct/dedup) — candidate: a distinct() or unique-cardinality helper.

GENUINE GAPS (await new capability, and each is a SCOPE decision against the narrowed DSL): (a) string-PREFIX operator for the naming rule 'a phase-kind member's id leads with the stage designator (phase-1a -> 1a-)'; (b) regex-VALIDITY check for 'branch patterns valid regexes'. NOTE: (a) is conditional (id-prefix depends on phase) so it is genuinely cross-field, but may be expressible in LAYER 1 via JSON Schema if/then + pattern rather than a DSL string op — worth checking before expanding the narrowed DSL. These are the real first-consumer requirements to validate the cross-file family design against (per DESIGN Cross-file semantics stay narrowed). Linked #60/#45.

95778743-832c-4111-af7f-a0a96d960566.author: 79Ig
95778743-832c-4111-af7f-a0a96d960566.content:
PASS — SECURITY + UNSAFE-CORRECTNESS (the load-bearing pass): list_dir handle-based rewrite in confine.rs. Audited the new unsafe FFI end-to-end; memory/resource safety and the confinement property both hold.

Resource discipline (list_dir_impl, confine.rs:408-481): validate_dir_chain now returns the leaf OwnedFd (:396-399, try_fold → final_dir) and enumeration runs through that exact handle — no re-resolution by path. `let raw = dir_fd.into_raw_fd()` (:419) releases OwnedFd ownership without closing; `fdopendir(raw)` (:420) adopts it on success. On the null branch errno is captured BEFORE `libc::close(raw)` (:422-424) so the diagnostic is not clobbered, and raw is closed exactly once (fdopendir does not adopt on failure) — no leak. On success DirGuard::drop → closedir(dirp) (:428-434) is the sole closer, created before any `?`, so closedir runs exactly once on every exit path (the loop `?` returns at :447/:460 included). No double-close: raw is never touched again after adoption. fstatat uses `dfd = dirfd(dirp)` (:435, :464), not the raw fd — POSIX-correct.

readdir loop (:438-479): errno is reset via errno_location() before each readdir (:442) so an error-NULL is distinguished from end-of-stream (:444-450) — no silent truncation. d_name read through CStr::from_ptr (:451), NUL-bounded; "." and ".." skipped (:452).

Confinement property: validate_dir_chain runs first and refuses a symlinked intermediate no-follow (O_NOFOLLOW|O_DIRECTORY, :335) before fdopendir is ever called. Each entry is classified via fstatat AT_SYMLINK_NOFOLLOW (:464) → S_IFLNK reported as EntryType::Symlink, never resolved. The TOCTOU is genuinely closed on the unix path: the old code re-resolved root.join(rel) via std::fs::read_dir; the new code enumerates the validated leaf fd and stats entries relative to dfd, so no component swapped after validation can redirect the read. Interleaving fstatat with readdir on the same fd is safe (stat does not move the stream offset).

EINTR retry loops (openat in open_confined_impl :159-165 and open_child_dir :337-343): retry only on EINTR, break on success or any other error; fail-closed — access is granted only by a legitimate successful no-follow open, never by the retry path.

9cec24c2-9a6a-41d5-8e59-69cb98b62998.author: 79Ig
9cec24c2-9a6a-41d5-8e59-69cb98b62998.content:
FIELD REPORT relayed from the vsdd-cli session (2026-07-20). Evidence: vsdd-cli #658/#665/#666, commits fdbc3dc3 + 8affbe56 on vsdd-cli main; pair-rule convention decision vsdd-cli #660.

CONTEXT: vsdd-cli's phase-1c data authoring ships every versioned data set as a PAIR — a frontmatter'd markdown registry artifact + a JSON Schema at .mdatron/schemas/<schema_class>.json — validated by 'mdatron verify' wired into pre-commit. Three sets landed (installed-artifact manifest, state schema, composition-scope + action vocabulary); the pair rule is now their standing convention.

THE LIVE CATCH (first real external catch): second landing, first attempt blocked at pre-commit by
  error[MDATRON-E0050]: frontmatter-schema-violation
    --> templates/registry/composition-scope-and-actions.md:1
     = note: Additional properties are not allowed ('adoption', 'sign-off)' were unexpected) (/action_vocabulary/20)
Root cause: a YAML flow-map member's human-text value contained commas — human: awaiting an operator act (ratification, adoption, sign-off) — so flow-map syntax split the text into stray keys. SEMANTIC corruption, not a format nit: display text bled into the data structure. Fixed by quoting; second run clean. Author never left the terminal.

WHAT IT VALIDATES: (1) the rustc-shaped diagnostic contract works — file, member path (JSON pointer), the unexpected keys named, and the explain code all present, fix in one pass; (2) JSON-Schema-over-frontmatter catches REAL bugs — additionalProperties:false was the load-bearing line; a permissive schema would have passed the corrupted member silently, and this vocabulary feeds convergence-test exact-match comparison, so a stray key would have surfaced much later as a convergence failure; (3) the adopter surface is right — .mdatron/schemas/ discovered and enforced with ZERO configuration, first try.

bc81359e-bf75-45ec-896a-418745373491.title: explain/DESIGN honesty: non-unix fallback's weaker TOCTOU posture overclaimed by E0012 page, uncarved in DESIGN.md
bc81359e-bf75-45ec-896a-418745373491.description: From the #52 Security domain review (SEC-F1, medium; Raise-to-SO — spec-contract change authority). The E0012 explain page (mdatron-cli/src/explain/MDATRON-E0012.md:15-17) states unqualified 'a link swapped after the check cannot redirect the read: the handle that passed confinement is the handle that is read.' On the non-unix fallback (confine.rs:165-185: symlink_metadata check, then a FOLLOWING File::open) that claim is false — a component swapped inside the check-to-open window is followed. The module docs are honest about this; the adopter-facing page and DESIGN.md § Verification is fast where it is invoked are not (no platform carve-out). This is exactly the check-then-read gap the snapshot model exists to close, reopened per-platform while the docs assert it closed. Resolution options: (a) qualify the explain page + record the platform carve-out in DESIGN.md via SO sign-off, or (b) implement a handle-based Windows walk (FILE_FLAG_OPEN_REPARSE_POINT) and delete the fallback's weakness. Owner: security domain, routed to solution-owner for the spec decision. Validator: red-team; the falsification is textual (read the three texts side by side) plus invariant I11 from the #52 record.

32a217cf-8e53-4440-9cc4-2e31a7dce999.author: IMWa
32a217cf-8e53-4440-9cc4-2e31a7dce999.content:
TECHNICAL WRITER review of 2646bbb: PASS — the qualification is comprehensible to an adopter who hits E0012, and the page stays internally coherent.

Comprehensibility: the platform split is explicit and actionable. Page 18-26 names the primitive (openat + O_NOFOLLOW), states the Unix guarantee, then names the fallback behavior ("a component swapped between the check and the open is followed") and its bound ("the swap-proof guarantee is Unix-only"). An adopter does not need to read confine.rs to know the posture on their platform. The How-to-fix (replace the symlink with the real file, or repoint the source) is unchanged and correct on both platforms, so the qualification does not disturb the remediation path — the reason the diagnostic fired is still fully explained.

Internal coherence (the specific check the manifest asks for — unconditional refusal rationale vs platform-split guarantee): the two rationales are cleanly separated and do not collide. The UNCONDITIONAL half is escape-detection: "Refusing symlinks unconditionally means escape detection never depends on resolving where a link points" (true on both platforms). The PLATFORM-SPLIT half is swap-proofness (Unix-only). The change correctly split the pre-existing single sentence so the unconditional clause and the conditional clause no longer share one predicate — before, both hung off one "and ... cannot redirect the read". The DESIGN.md cross-reference (§ Verification is fast where it is invoked) is retained and still resolves.

Minor (non-blocking): page 24-26 "That weaker fallback posture is the one place the snapshot model check-then-read closure is reopened, and it holds only until a handle-based walk lands" is dense and forward-leaning. It reads well for an engineer but leans on "holds only until", which promises more roadmap certainty than the module doc grants — see the red-team caveat comparing it to the module-doc "tolerated" framing (confine.rs:166-168).

34153b0e-25be-4f9b-9151-7e0118f4288d.author: 79Ig
34153b0e-25be-4f9b-9151-7e0118f4288d.content:
PROPOSED DESIGN.md CARVE-OUT (reconstructed — the container agent's draft never synced, #59). Raise-to-SO: recorded here for operator ratification; NOT applied to ratified text. Scope: § 'Verification is fast where it is invoked'. Three clause amendments:

[A1] Line 94, after '…do not affect the in-flight run's results.' — ADD: 'The no-follow handle read is the Unix implementation (openat/O_NOFOLLOW): the handle that passed confinement is the handle that is read, so a component swapped after the check cannot redirect the read. On platforms without a handle-relative no-follow open, the fallback performs a per-component symlink check followed by an ordinary open, and a component swapped inside that check-to-open window is followed — a weaker TOCTOU posture the contract tolerates only where the platform offers no openat surface, and only until a handle-based walk lands there. This is the one place the snapshot model's check-then-read closure is reopened, per-platform.'

[A2] Line 102 Falsification — REPLACE the trailing clause 'or a confinement check passed on a path that an interleaved swap redirects before the read.' WITH: 'or, on a platform providing handle-relative no-follow open, a confinement check passed on a path that an interleaved swap redirects before the read — the fallback platforms' documented check-to-open window is the carved-out exception above, not a falsification, until a handle-based walk lands.'

[A3] Line 104 Evidence — REPLACE '…while an agent concurrently edits — the snapshot model closes it),' WITH: '…while an agent concurrently edits — the snapshot model closes it on platforms with handle-relative no-follow open; see the platform carve-out for the fallback's tolerated weaker posture),'.

CONSEQUENTIAL (flag, operator's call whether to include now or defer): line 137's verification-architecture criterion 'a check-then-swap seed against confinement' is stated universally; under this carve-out it is a Unix criterion, with the fallback platforms exempt (documented xfail) until a handle-based walk lands. 4cec985f-0ba1-4d11-8b5b-797499b1212d.author: IMWa
4cec985f-0ba1-4d11-8b5b-797499b1212d.content:
SECURITY review of 2646bbb: PASS — SEC-F1 overclaim corrected; current per-platform claims are accurate against confine.rs.

Checked the page against confine.rs open_confined_impl (unix cfg 92-163; non-unix cfg 169-185) and DESIGN.md 92-104.

1. Platform-independent claim TRUE on both platforms. Page line 15-16 "escape detection never depends on resolving where a link points" holds: unix refuses via O_NOFOLLOW (confine.rs:110) and non-unix via symlink_metadata().file_type().is_symlink() (confine.rs:175) — neither resolves the link target.

2. Swap-proof claim now correctly Unix-scoped. Page 18-21 "Where the platform provides a handle-relative no-follow open — Unix, via `openat` with `O_NOFOLLOW` — a link swapped after the check cannot redirect the read: the handle that passed confinement is the handle that is read" matches the unix handle walk exactly (openat_no_follow returns the leaf OwnedFd that becomes the read File, confine.rs:161-162). It is no longer asserted unconditionally — that was the whole of SEC-F1.

3. Reopened check-to-open window described accurately vs the code. Page 21-23 "a per-component symlink check followed by an ordinary open, so a component swapped between the check and the open is followed" is a faithful description of confine.rs:171-184: a per-component symlink_metadata loop, then a single FOLLOWING File::open(&current) on the joined path. "the swap-proof guarantee is Unix-only" is correct: the fallback still refuses symlinks in the non-racing case (each component is stat-checked no-follow) but is not swap-proof under a race.

RESIDUAL (noted, not a blocker; belongs with the DESIGN.md carve-out, not this doc-honesty commit): the first paragraph still frames the mechanism universally — page 11-12 "reads adopter-supplied paths through validated no-follow handles, resolved per path component". On the non-unix fallback there is no validated-handle read: symlink_metadata validates the PATH, then File::open follows that path, so the validated artifact is not the read artifact — which is precisely the gap. This paragraph asserts no swap-proofness (that clause was relocated to the Unix paragraph) and its stated conclusion (symlink refused at any depth, non-racing) is true on both platforms, so it is not an independent NEW overclaim. It mirrors DESIGN.md:94 "confinement decided on the handle" and should be tightened together with that ratified text under the operator carve-out, not unilaterally here.

d3aed170-b1a6-459b-8b12-d7b209f28321.content:
RED-TEAM review of 2646bbb: PASS on current-safety disclosure — could not falsify the shipped claim. Two framing caveats + one out-of-scope residual recorded for the driver/operator.

Falsification attempt on CURRENT behavior — FAILED to falsify (the good outcome): no reading of page 18-23 lets an adopter believe the shipped non-unix code is swap-proof. "the swap-proof guarantee is Unix-only" and "a component swapped between the check and the open is followed" are unambiguous and match confine.rs:169-185.

Three-texts side-by-side (per manifest step 3):
- confine.rs module docs (1-21) + non-unix fallback comment (165-168): honest — "Weaker than the unix handle walk (a component swapped between the check and the open is not caught), which the snapshot contract tolerates only where the platform offers no `openat` surface." AGREES with the page on the existence and direction of the gap.
- E0012 page (post-2646bbb): honest — discloses Unix-only swap-proofness. AGREES.
- DESIGN.md § Verification is fast where it is invoked: NOT carved. Line 94 still asserts unconditionally "reads its inputs once, through validated no-follow handles with confinement decided on the handle", and the falsification clause at line 102 ("a confinement check passed on a path that an interleaved swap redirects before the read") is currently TRIPPED by the shipped non-unix fallback. This is the "uncarved in DESIGN.md" half of #56 and is expected: the [plan] scoped this commit to the page and DRAFTED the carve-out for operator ratification (Raise-to-SO). Per driver confirmation, that draft did NOT sync to the tracker — the authoring container agent (Qx3p-ZseB) could not run crosslink sync in-container (#59) — so no decision-kind carve-out comment exists on #56 to place side-by-side; treat it as operator-pending-and-unavailable. NET: two of three texts agree and are honest; DESIGN.md remains the open remediation and #56 cannot fully close until the operator ratifies the carve-out.

Caveat 1 (tension between the two AGREEING texts): page 25-26 "it holds only until a handle-based walk lands on those platforms" frames the gap as TEMPORARY and forthcoming; the module doc (166-168) frames it as "tolerated ... where the platform offers no `openat` surface" — conditional and possibly permanent. No Windows handle walk is committed: the [plan] defers it and resolution option (a) (accept the carve-out) is live. Falsifying reading: an adopter infers Windows swap-proofness is on the roadmap, which the code and the current routing do not guarantee. This is roadmap-certainty only, not a current-safety overclaim. Recommend softening "holds only until" toward the module-doc "tolerated" framing when the DESIGN.md carve-out is drafted for ratification.

Caveat 2: first-paragraph mechanism phrasing "validated no-follow handles, resolved per path component" (page 11-12) still reads as universal; the non-unix fallback uses symlink_metadata + a following open, not a validated-handle read. Same residual flagged in the security pass; fold into the DESIGN.md carve-out (DESIGN.md:94 carries the identical "decided on the handle" wording).

Invariant note: the seam this text documents is the check-then-swap seam (I5 in the #52 security record), listed there as UNTESTED and routed to the phase-2a fixture-corpus feed (#52 leg 5). The honesty fix documents the seam but adds no fixture, so I5 stays open — the non-unix TOCTOU window has prose disclosure but no red-gate test pinning it. Not a defect in this doc-only commit; flagged so the driver lane does not read #56 as retiring that debt.

2db9bd3e-1b2a-458e-b4b3-832f35213815.author: 79Ig
2db9bd3e-1b2a-458e-b4b3-832f35213815.content: Signing breakage confirmed after this session's worktree cleanup — corroborates upstream dollspace-gay/crosslink #610. Removing the merged kickoff worktrees left the hub-cache's WORKTREE-SCOPED git config (extensions.worktreeConfig=true) user.signingkey pointing at a deleted subagent key (.worktrees/Qx3p-wUhN-.../.crosslink/keys/ohMM_ed25519), so EVERY subsequent tracker write failed with 'Couldn't load public key … No such file or directory'. The driver key (79Ig, .crosslink/keys/79Ig_ed25519) was intact; only the pointer was dangling. FIX: git -C .crosslink/.hub-cache config --worktree user.signingkey <abs path to driver key>. Note the trap: a plain 'git config' (shared scope) does NOT override the --worktree value — must use --worktree. Candidate repro to add to upstream #610.

fa49e671-ea0b-4abd-b218-ddc1d39b5c8a.title: index: project_root interpolated unescaped into the glob pattern
fa49e671-ea0b-4abd-b218-ddc1d39b5c8a.description: From the #52 software-engineer domain review (SE-F2, major). resolve_source (mdatron-core/src/dsl/index.rs:178-180) builds the glob pattern as project_root.join(rel).to_string_lossy(), so glob metacharacters in the ROOT path are interpreted as pattern syntax: a root like /Users/x/proj[1] parses [1] as a char class matching 1 — every glob source enumerates the SIBLING tree /Users/x/proj1/... and legitimate in-root globs match nothing; sibling matches then fail strip_prefix and surface as PathTraversal carrying the sibling's paths. Deny-by-default holds (no content read), but in-root globs silently break for such roots. Also folds in SE-F5: to_string_lossy degrades non-UTF8 roots to U+FFFD before matching. Fix direction: glob::Pattern::escape the root portion, or enumerate relative to the root. Owner: software-engineer domain. Validator: sanity-check pair with the falsifying fixture: root containing [1] in a component + source *.yaml + matching in-root file — the file is not found today.

fbaae8f9-41ac-498e-ae2e-019a69bd0b14.title: index: glob enumeration semantics unpinned — closed-world discipline not enforced or tested
fbaae8f9-41ac-498e-ae2e-019a69bd0b14.description: From the #52 SE + Security domain reviews (SE-F3 major / SEC-F2 medium, converged). DESIGN.md § Five check families requires closed-world walking: symlinks are not followed during enumeration and symlink cycles cannot extend a walk. resolve_source delegates expansion to glob::glob (mdatron-core/src/dsl/index.rs:174-197), whose follow/no-follow and cycle behavior is neither pinned by the code nor exercised by any test — the only glob-symlink test (glob_matched_symlink_refused) covers a symlink at the LEAF of a match, not enumeration THROUGH a symlinked directory or a cycle. Exposure if glob follows: directory-listing disclosure outside the governed tree (filenames into diagnostics via match paths) and unbounded walk on a cycle (hook-time DoS; the design's step budget is not yet implemented on this path). Also carries SEC-F3 (low): the strip_prefix failure branch puts full out-of-root matched paths into PathTraversal message text unmarked. Fix direction: no-follow bounded walk (the same primitive the extras scan needs) + the I9/I10 fixtures from the #52 invariants; render out-of-root paths under the marking discipline. Owner: software-engineer domain with platform-engineer (dependency behavior). Validator: red-team, via two fixtures: (a) source sub/*.yaml with sub a symlink to an outside dir; (b) in-root symlink cycle under a recursive pattern — observe enumeration and termination. Feeds the phase-2a fixture-corpus audit.

2a4e4b91-a7cc-46bc-b9b4-f650ed1ebc44.author: ohMM
2a4e4b91-a7cc-46bc-b9b4-f650ed1ebc44.content:
RED-TEAM review of 9957464: PASS — both plan fixtures are genuinely pinned and I could not construct a disclosing or non-terminating enumeration against the walk (modulo the security pass's noted TOCTOU race).

Fixture (a) sub/*.yaml, sub -> outside dir holding SECRET-OUTSIDE.yaml: symlinked_intermediate_enumeration_refused_without_disclosure builds it and asserts BOTH the structural refusal (IndexError::SymlinkRefused{component:'sub'}) and the disclosure guard (!msg.contains('SECRET-OUTSIDE')). The two-part assertion is what makes it a real pin rather than a smoke test.

Fixture (b) in-root loop -> root under **/*.yaml: symlink_cycle_under_recursive_pattern_terminates runs the build on a worker thread with a 10s recv_timeout watchdog, asserts len==1 / lookup('found'). An unbounded walk fails by timeout rather than hanging CI — correct construction.

Sanity-check of the driver-lane '3 of 5 red on macOS by reverting resolve_source to glob::glob' claim against the test bodies — PLAUSIBLE and internally consistent:
 - metachar_in_root: root dir 'metachar-[1]'; glob::glob over '.../metachar-[1]/*.yaml' parses '[1]' as a one-char class, so the literal dir isn't matched -> idx.len()==0, tripping assert_eq!(len,1). RED.
 - symlinked_intermediate: glob::glob follows 'sub', matches SECRET-OUTSIDE.yaml, strip_prefix succeeds, open refuses 'sub' -> SymlinkRefused whose path text contains 'SECRET-OUTSIDE' -> disclosure assert trips. RED.
 - symlink_cycle: default glob follows loop/loop/loop... -> exceeds the 10s watchdog. RED.
 - non_utf8: Linux-only; skips on macOS APFS (EILSEQ). NOT red on macOS.
 - out_of_root marking: reverting only resolve_source leaves escape_path_text intact, so the PathTraversal message still escapes -> stays GREEN.
 That yields exactly 3 red on macOS. The figure is coherent.

Only enumeration path that can reach out-of-tree names is through a symlink, and validate_dir_chain refuses it no-follow before read; the sole residual is the check-then-read-by-path TOCTOU flagged in the security pass.

2bb135ee-7ca9-466b-b4bb-813990596de4.author: 79Ig
2bb135ee-7ca9-466b-b4bb-813990596de4.content: Consolidation (fix-lane rule: symptoms sharing one root cause consolidate before the fix; one owning finding per fix commit). This issue absorbs #54: both defects are symptoms of one root cause — resolve_source delegates expansion to glob::glob over a TEXTUALLY-JOINED ABSOLUTE pattern instead of an engine-owned, root-relative, no-follow, bounded walk. Survivor symptom list: (1) glob metacharacters in the root path parse as pattern syntax — sibling-tree enumeration, in-root globs silently broken (from #54); (2) non-UTF8 roots degraded via to_string_lossy before matching (from #54/SE-F5); (3) enumeration follow/no-follow semantics unpinned against the closed-world discipline — symlinked-intermediate traversal disclosure (SEC-F2); (4) cycle termination unproven — unbounded-walk DoS (I10); (5) out-of-root match paths surface unmarked in PathTraversal messages (SEC-F3). Pin tests the gate must run red-then-green: metachar-root fixture ([1] in a root component + in-root *.yaml match), non-UTF8-root fixture (unix), symlinked-intermediate-enumeration fixture (sub/*.yaml with sub linked outside), cycle-termination fixture (in-root symlink loop under a recursive pattern), marking fixture for the out-of-root message path.

3ba1e318-1568-440e-a300-ab5fba5e5b41.author: ohMM
3ba1e318-1568-440e-a300-ab5fba5e5b41.content:
SOFTWARE ENGINEER review of 9957464: PASS — the engine-owned walk correctly implements closed-world enumeration and structurally closes all five absorbed symptoms.

Closed-world walk (dsl/index.rs walk_segments): terminal segments select existing non-dir entries; Wildcard/Recursive descent only enters entries where 'entry.file_type == EntryType::Dir', and list_dir classifies a symlink as EntryType::Symlink (never its target's type), so a symlinked directory is never descended — a symlink cycle 'cannot extend the walk' (confirmed by symlink_cycle_under_recursive_pattern_terminates, len==1 under a 10s watchdog). The '**' arm tries rest at the current prefix then recurses real dirs only.

ConfinedPath re-confinement is sound and TOTAL: 'out' holds prefix.join(name) built solely from Normal components — entry names come from DirEntry::file_name() and std::fs::read_dir skips '.'/'..', so no path can carry a '..' or root segment; confine_lexically therefore always succeeds, and the defensive PathTraversal map is unreachable-but-guarded. Each match is still re-opened via open_confined (handle-based no-follow), so a glob-matched symlink LEAF still errors SymlinkRefused (established contract, glob_matched_symlink_refused).

Bounds enforced in list_for_walk: depth (Normal-component count of prefix) > MAX_WALK_DEPTH(64) and a CUMULATIVE entries_seen > MAX_WALK_ENTRIES(100_000) each raise WalkBounded. No unwrap/expect/panic on adversarial input in non-test code (classify_segment maps non-UTF8 and Pattern::new errors to IndexError; try_fold over an empty component list is lint-clean).

Minor (non-blocking) notes: (1) a literal intermediate that is a regular FILE yields Io('not a directory') rather than glob's 'no matches' — defensible under no-silent-degradation, but a behavior change from glob::glob; (2) '**/**/*.yaml' can push duplicate PathBufs (collapse harmlessly in the BTreeMap key space); (3) depth is only checked where a directory is listed, so a pure-literal chain skips the check — bounded by pattern length regardless.

d23b9cde-27a0-484d-ad22-b749d6e0a51a.author: ohMM
d23b9cde-27a0-484d-ad22-b749d6e0a51a.content:
SECURITY review of 9957464: PASS on the owning findings (SEC-F2 + SEC-F3), with two low-severity residuals to route.

SEC-F2 (no enumeration THROUGH a symlinked intermediate) — CLOSED. list_dir runs validate_dir_chain FIRST (unix: openat O_NOFOLLOW|O_DIRECTORY threaded down each component) so 'sub' in 'sub/*.yaml' is refused as ListViolation::Symlink before any content is read; list_for_walk maps it to SymlinkRefused. Pinned by symlinked_intermediate_enumeration_refused_without_disclosure, which asserts component=='sub' AND !msg.contains("SECRET-OUTSIDE").

SEC-F3 (out-of-root path unmarked in a strip_prefix message) — CLOSED. The strip_prefix branch is gone: the walk emits only root-relative Normal-component paths, so no out-of-root path is ever produced. The confinement rejections that DO carry adopter text (AbsoluteSource, PathTraversal, SymlinkRefused, Io) all now route through escape_path_text, which is byte-identical to diagnostic::Location::safe_display (is_control -> \\x{:02X}). Pinned by out_of_root_path_in_message_is_marked (ESC/LF/DEL render as \\x1B/\\x0A/\\x7F, no raw control byte survives).

Symptom-4 cycle termination — CLOSED (no-follow descent, see SE pass).

RESIDUAL 1 (TOCTOU, low): list_dir proves the chain symlink-free on HANDLES, then reads by PATH via std::fs::read_dir(root.join(rel)) — reintroducing exactly the check-then-use window open_confined's handle discipline was built to eliminate (module doc: 'decided on the handle, not on a path that could be swapped between check and read'). Static case is fully closed/pinned; exploit needs an adopter racing their own tree mid-hook, and a raced-in symlinked leaf is still refused at open_confined. Recommend an fdopendir-based read for parity.

RESIDUAL 2 (marking completeness, low): the doc claims 'adopter path text that does reach a message is escaped', but IndexError::Glob built in classify_segment (pattern: s.to_string() / name.to_string_lossy()) and IndexError::WalkBounded (pattern: source.to_string()) do NOT run through escape_path_text. Falsifying path: source '[\u{1b}.yaml' is a glob, confines lexically, then glob::Pattern::new fails on the unclosed class -> IndexError::Glob Display emits the raw ESC. Glob non-escape is largely inherited from the old delegation; WalkBounded is new in this commit. Neither reopens SEC-F2/F3, but the blanket claim is overbroad — route to #57/marking-completeness.

f3e4f2f6-8f97-4957-91a5-798d63eb567d.author: ohMM
f3e4f2f6-8f97-4957-91a5-798d63eb567d.content:
PLATFORM ENGINEER review of 9957464: PASS — the glob-crate dependency is correctly demoted to a single-component matcher, and the no-follow handle semantics are consistent across platforms.

glob crate: no glob::glob traversal remains. glob::Pattern is used ONLY per-name — classify_segment: glob::Pattern::new(s) for one component; pattern_matches: name.to_str().is_some_and(|s| pattern.matches(s)) against a single DirEntry name, which never contains '/'. So even though glob's default MatchOptions let '*' cross a separator, there is no separator in the matched string — traversal is entirely engine-owned (walk_segments). Only an exact '**' component becomes Segment::Recursive; 'a**' is a Wildcard matched per-name, not a recursive descent. The plan's dependency question is answered.

no-follow handles: unix validate_dir_chain opens each component via openat(O_RDONLY|O_NOFOLLOW|O_CLOEXEC|O_DIRECTORY) threaded from the root handle; the non-unix fallback stats each component with symlink_metadata (weaker/TOCTOU, explicitly documented, same tolerance open_confined carries). Both cfg arms present and mirror open_confined_impl. The macOS quirk is handled: with O_DIRECTORY, a symlink-to-dir returns ENOTDIR not ELOOP, so the code disambiguates with an AT_SYMLINK_NOFOLLOW fstatat checking S_IFLNK — byte-for-byte the same technique as open_confined. ELOOP/EMLINK (FreeBSD-lineage) also covered.

non_utf8 EILSEQ skip: LEGITIMATE, not a hidden gap. The defect (a non-UTF8 root degraded via to_string_lossy) can only exist where a non-UTF8 root can be CREATED; APFS/HFS+ reject it at mkdir with EILSEQ, so the fixture is unbuildable there. The guard skips ONLY on raw_os_error()==EILSEQ and panics on any other create error, and the structural fix (root carried as &Path/OsStr; entry names matched via to_str() -> None is a non-match, never lossy) holds regardless. Confirmed: the pin runs green here (macOS) as a skip; Linux runs it red-green for real.
